item.title: 【Introducing Pyroscope 2.0: faster, more cost-effective continuous profiling at scale】Pyrscope 2.0 简介：更快、更具成本效益的大规模连续分析
item.description:
【

Continuous profiling is becoming a standard part of the observability stack, and for good reason. It's the only signal that tells you why your code is slow or expensive, not just that it is. Metrics tell you CPU usage is high. Logs tell you a request was slow. Traces tell you which service is the bottleneck. But only a profile tells you which function, on which line, is burning the cycles.

As systems grow more complex, that level of visibility becomes essential. OpenTelemetry recently declared its Profiles signal as alpha, marking a clear step toward profiling becoming a first-class observability signal. 

Now, we’re also taking a next step with the release of Pyroscope 2.0, a ground-up rearchitecture of our open source continuous profiling database. It’s designed to make continuous profiling more cost-effective at scale, and with native support for OpenTelemetry Protocol (OTLP) profiling, you can start ingesting profiles using the emerging standard today.

The case for always-on profiling

Before we get into what's new in Pyroscope 2.0, it's worth talking about why continuous profiling matters, especially because the payoff is larger than most teams realize.

Cut infrastructure costs with data, not guesswork

Cloud spend is one of the biggest line items in engineering budgets, and a significant part of it is CPU and memory. Teams routinely overprovision because they don't have fine-grained visibility into what's actually consuming resources.

Continuous profiling changes that equation. When you can see exactly which functions are responsible for CPU and memory consumption—across every service, in production, over time—you can make targeted optimizations instead of throwing hardware at the problem. 

Faster root cause analysis

When an incident hits, the first question is always why. Metrics and traces narrow the blast radius; you know which service, which endpoint, and maybe which deployment introduced the regression. But the last mile of root cause analysis is where teams lose hours.

With continuous profiling, that last mile shrinks to minutes. You can compare a profile from before and after the regression, diff them, and see exactly which code paths changed. No reproducing in staging, no adding ad-hoc logging, and no guessing.

Understand latency at the code level

While distributed tracing tells you where wall clock time is spent, profiling tells you where the CPU spends that time. Together, they close the observability gap. A trace might show that your auth service added 200ms to a request, while a profile shows you that 150ms of that was in a regex compilation that could be cached.

This is especially powerful for tail latency, where the p99 spikes are hard to reproduce and harder to diagnose. Continuous profiling captures these moments as they happen, so you don't have to rely on luck with a debugger.

Pyroscope 2.0: a closer look at what’s new

The original Pyroscope architecture was based on Cortex, which is the same foundation that  the Mimir and Loki projects started with. It worked, but it carried overhead that made large-scale continuous profiling expensive to run and operationally heavy.

All three projects have since outgrown that foundation. Mimir recently redesigned its architecture to eliminate write-path replication, decouple reads from writes, and make object storage the single source of truth. Pyroscope 2.0 applies similar architectural principles, adapted for the unique characteristics of profiling data: large payloads, heavy symbolic information, and bursty query patterns. The result is a system that's dramatically cheaper, faster, and simpler to operate.

Profiling at scale without the cost penalty

The v1 architecture for Pyroscope replicated every profile three times on the write path. For a signal where a single profile can be tens of megabytes, that 3x amplification added up fast. Pyroscope 2.0 eliminates write-path replication entirely, so each profile is written exactly once to object storage.

But the bigger win is data co-location. Profiles from the same service now are stored close together, which means symbolic information like function names, source locations, and stack traces—which are often 60% or more of a profile's size—is deduplicated and kept within as few objects as required by the service’s data volume. In our production environment, this reduced the symbol storage footprint by up to 95%.

For teams that avoided continuous profiling because of storage and compute costs, these architectural changes make it practical to run profiling at scale.

Query performance that matches the workflow

Profiling queries are inherently expensive due to the sheer volume of data involved. Each pod continuously emits stack trace samples, so querying 100 pods over 12 hours means scanning and merging hundreds of millions of samples, which can require hundreds of CPU-seconds of processing. 

In v1, this work happened inside stateful components that couldn't scale elastically; you had to reserve capacity for peak query load, even if that capacity sat idle 99% of the time.

Pyroscope 2.0 makes the entire read path stateless. Any querier can process any query, and queriers scale up and down based on demand. You pay for query compute when you're actually querying instead of all the time.

This matters because profiling has a bursty access pattern. There is essentially no base load; nobody is polling profiles on a dashboard every 30 seconds. But when an incident happens, multiple engineers start running heavy queries simultaneously. And increasingly, LLM-powered agents are querying profiling data autonomously as part of automated investigations, adding significant traffic. With stateless queriers, the system can handle these spikes gracefully without paying for idle capacity the rest of the time.

Operational simplicity

Fewer stateful components means fewer things that can break and faster recovery when they do. Rollouts that took 8-12 hours in v1 now complete in minutes. The segment writer is diskless. The store-gateway is gone. The operational surface area is significantly smaller.

For teams running Pyroscope themselves, this is the difference between "we need a dedicated person to operate this" and "it just runs."

Pressure-tested in Grafana Cloud

Grafana Cloud Profiles, our hosted continuous profiling tool powered by Pyroscope, has been running Pyroscope 2.0 in production since April 2025. We rolled it out to every region by September, and have since processed 19.5PB of profiling data. The challenges we set out to fix, including wasteful replication, coupled read/write paths, and slow rollouts, are measurably gone.

If you're a Grafana Cloud Profiles user, the migration has already happened. This release brings the same production-proven architecture to the open source community.

A foundation for new capabilities

Beyond the operational improvements, the cleaner architecture in Pyroscope 2.0 enables features that simply weren't feasible in v1, including:

  • Metrics from profiles: aggregate profiling data into fleet-wide metrics to compare resource consumption across services, versions, or deployments without querying individual profiles.
  • Individual profile inspection: drill into a single profile instance rather than only viewing aggregates.
  • Heatmap queries (shown below): visualize profile distributions over time to spot patterns and outliers.
  • Richer query types: the stateless read path and cleaner data model make it possible to build new analysis capabilities without touching every component in the system.

Getting started

Pyroscope 2.0 is available now. If you're upgrading from v1, the key change is that object storage is required for distributed deployments, as it's the single source of truth for all profile data. 

For step-by-step migration instructions, please reference our Pyroscope 2.0 migration guide. You can also learn more in our release notes.

To learn more about all the announcements coming out of GrafanaCON 2026, read our GrafanaCON announcements blog post. 

】

连续分析正在成为可观察性堆栈的标准部分，这是有充分理由的。这是唯一告诉您代码为何缓慢或昂贵的信号，而不仅仅是事实本身。指标告诉您 CPU 使用率很高。日志告诉您请求很慢。跟踪告诉您哪个服务是瓶颈。但只有一个配置文件可以告诉您哪个功能、哪条线路正在消耗周期。

随着系统变得越来越复杂，这种可见性级别变得至关重要。 OpenTelemetry 最近宣布其 Profiles 信号为 alpha，标志着分析向一流可观测信号迈出了明确的一步。 

现在，我们还发布了Pyrscope 2.0，这是我们的开源连续分析数据库的彻底重新架构。它旨在使连续分析在规模上更具成本效益，并且通过对开放遥测协议 (OTLP) 分析的本机支持，您可以开始使用当今新兴标准提取配置文件。

始终在线分析的案例

在我们了解 Pyrscope 2.0 的新增功能之前，值得讨论一下为什么连续分析很重要，特别是因为回报比大多数团队都大实现。

通过数据而非猜测来削减基础设施成本

云支出是工程预算中最大的项目之一，其中很大一部分是 CPU 和内存。团队经常会过度配置，因为他们无法详细了解实际消耗资源的情况。

持续的分析会改变这种情况。当您可以准确地了解哪些功能导致了 CPU 和内存消耗（跨每个服务、在生产中、随着时间的推移），您可以进行有针对性的优化，而不是用硬件来解决问题。 

更快的根本原因分析

当事件发生时，第一个问题始终是为什么。指标和痕迹缩小了爆炸半径；您知道哪个服务、哪个端点以及可能哪个部署引入了回归。但根本原因分析的最后一英里是团队浪费时间的地方。

通过持续分析，最后一英里可以缩短到几分钟。您可以比较回归前后的配置文件，比较它们，并准确查看哪些代码路径发生了变化。无需在分段中进行复制，无需添加临时日志记录，也无需猜测。

了解代码级别的延迟

虽然分布式跟踪会告诉您挂钟时间都花在哪里，但分析会告诉您 CPU 将时间花在哪里。它们共同缩小了可观察性的差距。跟踪可能显示您的身份验证服务向请求添加了 200 毫秒uest，而配置文件显示其中 150 毫秒位于可以缓存的正则表达式编译中。

这对于尾部延迟尤其强大，其中 p99 峰值难以重现且难以诊断。连续分析会在这些时刻发生时捕获这些时刻，因此您不必依靠调试器的运气。

Pyrscope 2.0：仔细了解新功能

原始 Pyrscope 架构基于 Cortex，它与 Mimir 和 Loki 项目开始。它有效，但它带来的开销使得大规模连续分析的运行成本高昂且操作繁重。

所有三个项目后来都超出了该基础。 Mimir 最近重新设计了其架构，以消除写入路径复制，将读取与写入分离，并使对象存储成为单一事实来源。 Pyrscope 2.0 采用类似的架构原理，适应分析数据的独特特征：大有效负载、大量符号信息和突发查询模式。结果是一个更便宜、更快、操作更简单的系统。

大规模分析而不会造成成本损失

Pyrscope 的 v1 架构在写入路径上将每个配置文件复制了 3 次。对于单个轮廓可能达到数十兆字节的信号来说，3 倍的放大速度加起来很快。 Pyrscope 2.0 完全消除了写入路径复制，因此每个配置文件都只写入对象存储一次。

但更大的好处是数据共置。来自同一服务的配置文件现在紧密存储在一起，这意味着函数名称、源位置和堆栈跟踪等符号信息（通常占配置文件大小的 60% 或更多）会进行重复数据删除，并保存在服务数据量所需的尽可能少的对象内。在我们的生产环境中，这将符号存储占用空间减少了高达 95%。

对于因存储和计算成本而避免连续分析的团队来说，这些架构更改使得大规模运行分析变得可行。

与工作流程匹配的查询性能

由于涉及的数据量巨大，分析查询本质上是昂贵的。每个 Pod 不断发出堆栈跟踪样本，因此在 12 小时内查询 100 个 Pod 意味着扫描和合并数亿个样本，这可能需要数百个 CPU 秒的处理。 

在 v1 中，这项工作发生在无法弹性扩展的有状态组件内；你有o 为峰值查询负载保留容量，即使该容量在 99% 的时间内处于空闲状态。

Pyrscope 2.0 使整个读取路径无状态。任何查询器都可以处理任何查询，并且查询器可以根据需求进行扩展和缩减。您在实际查询时支付查询计算费用，而不是一直进行查询。

这很重要，因为分析具有突发访问模式。基本上没有基本负载；没有人每 30 秒在仪表板上轮询一次配置文件。但当事件发生时，多个工程师开始同时运行大量查询。作为自动化调查的一部分，法学硕士支持的代理越来越多地自主查询分析数据，从而增加了大量流量。借助无状态查询器，系统可以优雅地处理这些峰值，而无需为其余时间的闲置容量付费。

操作简单性

有状态组件越少，意味着可能损坏的东西越少，恢复速度也越快。在 v1 中需要 8-12 小时才能完成的部署现在只需几分钟即可完成。段写入器是无盘的。商店网关消失了。操作表面积明显更小。

对于自行运行 Pyrscope 的团队来说，这就是“我们需要专人来操作它”和“它只是运行”之间的区别。

在 Grafana Cloud 中进行压力测试

Grafana Cloud Profiles，我们托管的持续分析工具，由我们提供支持Pyrscope 自 2025 年 4 月以来一直在生产环境中运行 Pyrscope 2.0。到 9 月份，我们已将其推广到每个区域，此后已处理了 19.5PB 的分析数据。我们着手解决的挑战，包括浪费的复制、耦合的读/写路径和缓慢的推出，已经明显消失。

如果您是 Grafana Cloud Profiles 用户，迁移已经发生。此版本为开源社区带来了相同的经过生产验证的架构。

新功能的基础

除了操作改进之外，Pyrscope 2.0 中更简洁的架构还支持 v1 中根本不可行的功能，包括：

  • 配置文件中的指标：将分析数据聚合到整个队列的指标中，以比较跨服务、版本或部署的资源消耗，而无需查询各个服务、版本或部署
  • 单独的配置文件检查：深入了解单个配置文件实例，而不是仅查看聚合。
  • 热图查询（如下所示）：可视化配置文件随时间的分布，以发现模式和异常值。
  • 更丰富的查询类型：无状态读取路径和更清晰的数据模型使构建新的分析功能成为可能，而无需触及配置文件中的每个组件

开始使用

Pyrscope 2.0现已推出。如果您从 v1 升级，关键的变化是分布式部署需要对象存储，因为它是所有配置文件数据的单一事实来源。 

有关分步迁移说明，请参考我们的Pyrscope 2.0 迁移指南。您还可以在我们的发行说明中了解更多信息。

要了解有关 GrafanaCON 2026 的所有公告的更多信息，请阅读我们的GrafanaCON 公告博文。

item.title: 【Introducing o11y-bench: an open benchmark for AI agents running observability workflows】推出 o11y-bench：运行可观察性工作流程的 AI 代理的开放基准
item.description:
【

Evaluating agents is hard. Verifying observability tasks is harder.

Yes, AI agents have gotten dramatically and quantifiably better at coding and tool use, but observability presents a different kind of challenge. In a real incident, the hard part is rarely just writing a query. It's deciding which signal matters, figuring out whether a spike is noise or symptom, correlating metrics with logs and traces, and sometimes making a change in Grafana without breaking the dashboard another engineer depends on.

To help the Grafana community navigate this new world of AI-assisted observability, we’re open sourcing grafana/o11y-bench, a benchmark for evaluating AI agents on observability workflows. It runs agents against a real Grafana stack with access to Grafana MCP server and grades them on a set of observability tasks within that environment.

o11y-bench is built on Harbor, an open source framework released by the creators of Terminal Bench that standardizes environments for benchmarking agents against sets of focused tasks. The benchmark we developed focuses on the workflows that actually matter in practice: querying metrics, logs, and traces; investigating incidents; and making targeted dashboard changes.

Why observability needs its own benchmark

Observability isn't just another straightforward agent tool-calling problem. Observability tasks such as root-cause investigations or dashboard creation often depend on the interaction between large amounts of metrics, logs, traces, time ranges, and saved application state. And that collection of variables makes it harder to tell whether an agent actually got the work right. For example, a query can be syntactically valid and still select the wrong series; a dashboard can render and still be saved incorrectly.

To properly evaluate AI systems today, benchmark tasks and simulated environments must reflect reality. o11y-bench runs agents against a real Grafana stack and evaluates them on a set of focused criteria simulating the complexity of a modern observability stack.

This type of standardized measuring can provide critical insights for Grafana users because the outcome can help you discern the difference between an agent that looks helpful in a demo and one you can trust in a real workflow. In observability, the dangerous mistakes are often the subtle ones.

And by open sourcing the tasks, environment, grading logic, and results, we want this to be inspectable, reproducible, and open to challenge. We are also hoping that these tasks can help the next generation of models improve their observability related skills.

Open source, open testing

Built on Harbor, o11y-bench allows you to run your model, agent harness, or any combination of the two in a sandboxed environment alongside a Grafana Docker container with synthetic metrics, logs, and traces present. It’s as simple as running the following task to get started:

mise run bench:job -- --model openai/gpt-5.4-nano --task-name query-cpu-metrics --agent opencode

This command will kick off the benchmark over just one task, (query-cpu-metrics ) and output the results to the /jobs folder where you can inspect agent trajectory, see the LLM-as-a-judge and heuristic scoring, and understand how your agent or model performed. 

Our goal with o11y-bench is to engage the community to see what's possible. We have kicked off the leaderboard with a set of base frontier models, but we welcome new combinations of agent harnesses, model configurations, and experimentation to push agent capabilities in observability forward. 

What tasks o11y-bench tests 

The first public release of o11y-bench includes 63 tasks across various observability workflows:

  • Prometheus and PromQL tasks
  • Loki and LogQL tasks
  • Tempo and TraceQL tasks
  • Multi-step incident investigations
  • Dashboard editing and repair tasks

The tasks we have curated aim to be deterministic enough to grade reliably, but rich enough to produce real failure modes. For instance, take a problem from the Prometheus query category, promql-retry-backlog-triage:

“We think the payment incident may have built up retries behind the scenes. Over roughly the last six hours, which service showed the highest retry/backlog depth, about how high did it get, and does the next-worst service look like a smaller spillover or a comparable primary problem?”

To a human familiar with the system, this problem seems relatively straightforward. However, we noticed high-thinking or token-heavy agents would spin their wheels by gathering too much information about the system, wasting tokens and timing out. On the other hand, more-focused agents were able to zero-in on the proper queries and diagnose the system quickly and accurately. 

While a high-thinking agent may get there in the end, the metrics included with o11y-bench also allow us to examine cost, token usage, and overall performance rather than just a "0" or "1" answer, providing actionable insights on agents and models we may want to use for these types of scenarios.

Why verifying observability work is hard

Coming up with observability tasks that sufficiently test an agent is only part of the assessment process. You also need to be able to verify that those tasks are accurately completed.

If a user asks an agent to investigate latency, compare error rates, or update a dashboard, simply getting a final answer that looks good isn't good enough. For many query tasks, we run a reference Prometheus or Loki query against the same stack the agent saw, then compare that value to what the model actually cited. For dashboard tasks, we inspect the saved Grafana state and, when needed, execute the saved panel query and compare it against a reference query for the same case.

We start with outcomes. For the explanation itself, we still score the response, but we pair that with verifiable facts from the environment rather than treating fluent prose as evidence.

Two simple examples:

  • If a model says “the p95 latency was about 2.3 seconds,” the verifier can run the reference query against the same Prometheus or Loki data and check whether that number is actually supported.
  • If a model says it fixed a dashboard, the verifier can inspect the saved panel JSON, bind the expected variable values, execute the saved query, and compare the result to a reference query for the same case.

Our general grading philosophy is to always check against the ground truth of what the agent actually did, not just what it said. In practice, that is the difference between an agent that looks convincing in a transcript and one you can trust in a real investigation.

Measuring reliability vs. best-of-three success

Two headline scores are used in this benchmark to evaluate model performance:

  • Pass^3: A measure of consistency, computed as the average benchmark score across three runs
  • Pass@3: A measure of best-of-three success, indicating whether the model solved the task at least once across three attempts

Note: Each metric has its value, but their individual usefulness will depend on the use case. For the purposes of this exercise, we care more about consistency, so Pass^3 takes priority in the rankings. Further reading on agent eval methodology and metrics can be found on the Anthropic blog.

It's interesting to note how each model family performs in the relative metrics, as different leaders emerge when looking at the different metrics for success.

The results

The initial launch suite covered 29 model variants on 63 tasks (at three attempts each) for a total 5,481 total trials.

Using Pass^3 as the headline metric:

  • Opus 4.7 with reasoning turned off led the launch run
  • Opus 4.7 with reasoning set to high came in second, interestingly lower consistency at Pass^3 but with a higher Pass@3 score

Qwen 3.6 Plus performed the best of the frontier open source models we tested, even beating some of the smaller Sonnet and GPT models.

The main takeaway is that reliability is what truly separates the top models. Many models could get a task right at least once across three attempts. Far fewer could do it consistently. That gap is exactly why we treat reliability as the main benchmark signal. 

“Got it right once” and “gets it right consistently” are not the same thing, especially in observability, where a subtle mistake can send an engineer down the wrong path. Mean score is still useful for debugging tasks and graders, but it is not a good headline metric for agent trust.

A per-category view sharpened that picture further. Grafana API tasks were close to saturated, and Prometheus was relatively strong. Tempo and Loki sat in the middle. Dashboarding remains the hardest area, not because it is the only thing that matters, but because it combines state, query correctness, variable wiring, and saved behavior in ways that are easy to get almost right.

Here, Pass@3 means “got an individual task right at least once across three tries,” while a perfect Pass^3 means “got it right all three times.” The gap between those two is one of the main things the benchmark is trying to expose.

Try it yourself

The quickest way to get started is to head to the grafana/o11y-bench repo, clone it locally, and follow the README.

From there, you can run individual tasks, full suites, and comparison reports against any model or agent harness available through Harbor and LiteLLM.

If you try o11y-bench, we’d be interested to see how it holds up across more models, agent setups, and independent reproductions, as well as what it suggests for future benchmark revisions. Submit contributions to the HuggingFace leaderboard per the contributing guide or open an issue in the benchmark repo for feedback or discussions. 

For more information on this and all the other exciting updates from GrafanaCON 2026, check out our announcement blog for all the news. And for more information on Grafana Cloud AI, including FAQs about Assistant and our other AI capabilities, check out our AI observability page.

】

评估代理很困难。验证可观察性任务更加困难。

是的，人工智能代理在编码和工具使用方面已经取得了显着且可量化的进步，但可观察性提出了另一种挑战。在真实事件中，困难的部分很少只是编写查询。它决定哪个信号重要，确定尖峰是噪声还是症状，将指标与日志和跟踪关联起来，有时在 Grafana 中进行更改而不破坏其他工程师依赖的仪表板。

为了帮助 Grafana 社区驾驭这个人工智能辅助可观察性的新世界，我们正在开源 grafana/o11y-bench，用于在可观察性工作流程上评估人工智能代理的基准。它针对真实的 Grafana 堆栈运行代理，并可以访问 Grafana MCP 服务器，并根据该环境中的一组可观察性任务对它们进行评分。

o11y-bench 构建于 Harbor 之上，Harbor 的创建者发布了一个开源框架Terminal Bench，标准化针对一组重点任务对代理进行基准测试的环境。我们开发的基准侧重于实践中真正重要的工作流程：查询指标、日志和跟踪；调查事件；并进行有针对性的仪表板更改。

为什么可观察性需要自己的基准

可观察性不仅仅是另一个简单的代理工具调用问题。根本原因调查或仪表板创建等可观察性任务通常取决于大量指标、日志、跟踪、时间范围和保存的应用程序状态之间的交互。这些变量的集合使得判断代理是否真正正确完成了工作变得更加困难。例如，查询可能在语法上有效，但仍然选择了错误的系列；仪表板可以渲染，但仍然无法正确保存。

为了正确评估当今的人工智能系统，基准测试任务和模拟环境必须反映现实。 o11y-bench 针对真实的 Grafana 堆栈运行代理，并根据模拟现代可观测性堆栈复杂性的一组重点标准对其进行评估。

这种类型的标准化测量可以为 Grafana 用户提供重要的见解，因为结果可以帮助您辨别在演示中看起来有用的代理与在真实工作流程中值得信赖的代理之间的区别。在可观察性中，危险的错误往往是微妙的错误。

通过开源任务、环境、评分逻辑和结果，我们希望它是可检查的、可重现的，并且可以接受挑战。我们也希望这些任务能够帮助下一代模型提高可观测性相关技能。

开源、开放测试

基于Harbor构建，o11y-bench 让您能够您的模型、代理工具或两者的任意组合位于沙盒环境中，旁边是 Grafana Docker 容器，其中包含综合指标、日志和跟踪。只需运行以下任务即可开始：

mise run bench:job -- --model openai/gpt-5.4-nano --task-name query-cpu-metrics --agent opencode

此命令将仅对一项任务（query-cpu-metrics）启动基准测试，并将结果输出到 /jobs 文件夹，您可以在其中检查代理轨迹、查看 LLM 作为法官和启发式评分，并了解您的代理或模型的执行情况。 

我们 o11y-bench 的目标是让社区参与进来，看看有什么可能。我们已经通过一组基本前沿模型启动了排行榜，但我们欢迎智能体利用、模型配置和实验的新组合，以推动智能体在可观察性方面的能力向前发展。 

o11y-bench 测试的任务

o11y-bench 的第一个公开版本包括跨各种可观测性工作流程的63 个任务：

  • Prometheus 和 PromQL 任务
  • Loki 和 LogQL 任务
  • Tempo 和 TraceQL任务
  • 多步骤事件调查
  • 仪表板编辑和修复任务

我们策划的任务旨在具有足够的确定性以可靠地分级，但又足够丰富以产生真正的故障模式。例如，以 Prometheus 查询类别中的一个问题为例，promql-retry-backlog-triage：

“我们认为支付事件可能在幕后累积了重试。在大约过去的六个小时内，哪个服务显示了最高的重试/积压深度，关于如何它达到了多少，次差的服务看起来像是较小的溢出还是类似的主要问题？”

对于熟悉系统的人来说，这个问题似乎相对简单。然而，我们注意到高思维或大量代币的代理会通过收集太多有关系统的信息、浪费代币和超时而自转。另一方面，更专注的代理能够将正确的查询归零并快速准确地诊断系统。 

虽然高智商的代理最终可能会实现这一目标，但 o11y-bench 中包含的指标还允许我们检查成本、代币使用情况和整体性能，而不仅仅是“0”或“1”答案，从而为我们可能想要用于这些类型场景的代理和模型提供可操作的见解。

为什么验证可观察性工作很难

提出充分测试代理的可观察性任务只是其中的一部分评估过程。您还需要能够验证这些任务是否准确完成。

如果用户要求代理进行投资门延迟、比较错误率或更新仪表板，仅仅获得看起来不错的最终答案还不够。对于许多查询任务，我们针对代理看到的同一堆栈运行参考 Prometheus 或 Loki 查询，然后将该值与模型实际引用的值进行比较。对于仪表板任务，我们检查保存的 Grafana 状态，并在需要时执行保存的面板查询，并将其与同一案例的参考查询进行比较。

我们从结果开始。对于解释本身，我们仍然对响应进行评分，但我们将其与来自环境的可验证事实配对，而不是将流畅的散文视为证据。

两个简单的例子：

  • 如果模型说“p95延迟约为2.3秒”，验证者可以针对相同的Prometheus或Loki数据运行参考查询，并检查该数字是否确实受支持。
  • 如果模型说它修复了仪表板，验证者可以检查保存的面板 JSON，绑定预期的变量值，执行保存的查询，并将结果与相同案例的参考查询进行比较。

我们的一般评分理念是始终检查代理实际所做的事情的基本事实，而不仅仅是它所说的。实际上，这就是在文字记录中看起来令人信服的代理与在真实调查中值得信赖的代理之间的区别。

衡量可靠性与三局两胜的成功

此基准测试中使用两个标题分数来评估模型性能：

  • 通过^3：一致性度量，计算为三次运行的平均基准分数
  • 通过@3：衡量三局两胜的成功程度，表明模型是否在三次尝试中至少解决了一次任务

注意：每个指标都有其价值，但其各自的有用性将取决于用例。出于本练习的目的，我们更关心一致性，因此 Pass^3 在排名中优先。有关代理评估方法和指标的更多内容，请访问 Anthropic 博客。

值得注意的是，每个模型系列在相对指标中的表现如何，因为在查看不同的成功指标时会出现不同的领导者。

结果

最初的发布套件涵盖63 项任务的 29 个模型变体（每次尝试 3 次），总计 5,481 次试验。

使用 Pass^3 作为标题指标：

  • 关闭推理的 Opus 4.7 领先启动运行
  • Opus 4.7 推理设置为高 位居第二，有趣的是，一致性较低通过^3，但 Pass@3 得分更高

Qwen 3.6 Plus 在我们测试的前沿开源模型中表现最好，甚至击败了一些较小的 Sonnet 和 GPT 模型。

主要结论是可靠性才是顶级型号的真正区别。许多模型可以在三次尝试中至少一次正确完成任务。能够始终如一地做到这一点的人要少得多。这种差距正是我们将可靠性视为主要基准信号的原因。 

“一次就做对了”和“始终如一地做对了”不是一回事，尤其是在可观察性方面，一个微妙的错误可能会让工程师走上错误的道路。平均分数对于调试任务和评分者仍然有用，但它并不是代理信任的良好标题指标。

每个类别的视图进一步锐化了这一情况。 Grafana API任务接近饱和，Prometheus相对较强。坦波和洛基坐在中间。仪表板仍然是最难的领域，不是因为它是唯一重要的事情，而是因为它将状态、查询正确性、变量连接和保存的行为以很容易几乎正确的方式结合在一起。

这里，Pass@3 意味着“在三次尝试中至少一次正确完成单个任务”，而完美的 Pass^3 意味着“三次都正确”。这两者之间的差距是基准测试试图揭示的主要内容之一。

亲自尝试

最快的入门方法是前往 grafana/o11y-bench 存储库，将其克隆到本地，然后按照自述文件进行操作。

从那里，您可以运行单独的任务，完整的套件，以及针对 Harbor 和 LiteLLM 提供的任何模型或代理工具的比较报告。

如果您尝试 o11y-bench，我们很有兴趣了解它如何在更多模型、代理设置和独立复制品中保持不变，以及它对未来基准修订的建议。根据贡献指南向HuggingFace排行榜提交贡献，或在基准存储库用于反馈或讨论。 

有关此内容以及 GrafanaCON 2026 的所有其他令人兴奋的更新的更多信息，请查看我们的公告博客以获取所有新闻。有关 Grafana Cloud AI 的更多信息，包括有关 Assistant 和我们其他 AI 功能的常见问题解答，请查看我们的 AI 可观测性页面。

item.title: 【AI Observability in Grafana Cloud: A complete solution for monitoring your agentic workloads】Grafana Cloud 中的 AI 可观测性：用于监控代理工作负载的完整解决方案
item.description:
【

The observability industry has developed great tools for using metrics, logs, traces, and profiles to monitor the cloud native applications that have dominated the last decade of software development. 

But when it comes to understanding what an AI system is actually doing, we’re often left reading raw conversations, guessing at quality, and reacting too late. And that’s a problem. 

Agents make decisions, call tools, generate content, and interact with users, services, and applications in ways that traditional observability isn't designed to handle. As organizations shift from cloud native to AI native, it's increasingly clear that agent chats and sessions need to be treated as first-class signals alongside the rest of your more traditional telemetry. 

To address this emerging gap, we're launching AI Observability in Grafana Cloud. Available now in public preview, AI Observability actually started as an internal hackathon project designed to address some of our own agentic challenges. Since then, we've heard from lots of customers dealing with similar problems, so we decided to take what we learned and turn it into a complete solution for teams running agents in production, helping them understand what their AI is doing, how well it’s doing it, and where issues are emerging.

How AI Observability can help you today

Traditional observability gives you signals like CPU usage, request latency, and error rates. Those are important. But they don’t tell you if your agent is being helpful, hallucinating, or quietly degrading over time.

That's why we've built AI Observability in Grafana Cloud to help teams:

  • Observe AI agent behavior in real time, including inputs, outputs, and execution flows
  • Continuously evaluate outputs, with alerts for issues such as low-quality responses, policy violations, or anomalous behavior
  • Surface risk earlier, including potential data exposure or misuse (for example, leaked credentials or abnormal usage patterns)
  • Elevate agent sessions and conversations to first-class telemetry signals and correlate them in the same environment where applications are observed

AI Observability connects agents directly to traces, tool calls, token usage, costs, and (live) evaluations. And it does it all in the same Grafana Cloud environment where you observe the rest of your systems. 

This gives you true end-to-end signals—agentic or otherwise. So the next time something looks off, you won't just see a spike in latency. You'll also be able to open the exact conversation, inspect what happened, and understand why.

Instrument once, understand everything with open standards

AI Observability is OpenTelemetry-compatible, so it fits naturally into existing observability setups. You instrument your app once using a thin SDK, and AI Observability automatically captures:

  • Generations and conversations
  • Model and provider metadata
  • Tool usage
  • Latency and token metrics
  • Cost signals

From there, everything becomes queryable and explorable in one place. You can also filter by model or provider, time range, labels, or environments. This is particularly helpful if you have different providers, where the same model can function differently in different environments.

AI Observability automatically classifies and catalogs agent versions for you. If you change an agent’s system prompt or its tool set, a new agent version is created that you can inspect separately. This helps you find the best performing agent you have and spot specific problems agents might have.

Agents can break subtly; AI Observability gives you the context to know why

One of the hardest parts of running AI in production is that issues are often subtle. Nothing crashes. No alerts fire. But something is off and your users complain. Responses are getting longer and less useful, costs are creeping up, quality is slowly degrading, users are losing trust.

AI Observability is designed for this exact problem. You can drill into any conversation and see the full thread: tool calls and execution traces, token usage and cost breakdown, scores, ratings, and annotations. 

This is critical for debugging your agents. It helps you understand if specific agents struggle with specific models, or the impacts of your latest release. You can also know where your tokens are going—and, as a result, where your money is going. You can see if certain operations are expensive, or if certain tools are slow or struggle with certain tasks, which in turn adds to your costs.  

And if you want additional debugging support or advice for how to improve your agents, you can ask for help from Grafana Assistant using natural language. Because it can correlate your AI data with all your other telemetry signals, it can help all sorts of use cases. 

For example, it can see how much time you're spending on compute, or what's causing spikes in latency, and you can find out how that ties back to your AI. Essentially, it takes the power of our existing full-stack observability platform and extends it to the next-generation of applications your business relies on.

Get alerts when they matter

AI Observability can also help assess your AI's accuracy, which can become a major challenge when you have multiple agents running at scale. 

You can use LLM-as-a-judge, heuristics, or regex to detect undesirable outputs. And because AI Observability natively integrates with Grafana Alerting, you can get notified when your agents behave out of control. Taken collectively, this allows you to treat agents like the rest of your services and infrastructure.

You can even combine this with Assistant skills and agent-specific runbooks. That way, when you get paged about increased toxicity from your agent, you can ask Assistant to inspect the conversation, read your runbook, and offer remediation strategies.

AI Observability, from the team that built Grafana Assistant

We know about the challenge of observing agents because we've lived through it. When we started building Assistant, we looked at lots of frameworks, but they just weren’t detailed enough for how we wanted to monitor our agents.

We quickly realized we needed to build our own in-house solution. Assistant has been very well received by our users—so popular, in fact, that we're making it available in new and exciting ways—and a big part of that success has been our ability to closely monitor the feedback loop we built so we can keep track of how performance and customer behavior evolves. 

That success led us to think we should make this available to our customers. So, during a recent hackathon, the Assistant team took what it learned from monitoring agents, prompt engineering, keeping track of agent versions, handling tools, and more, and baked it into a solution that enables you to monitor agents at a large scale, too. 

As with Assistant, AI Observability is at the center of continuous innovation. We've already shipped new features, including user annotations and streamlined alerting, after working with customers during the private preview. And we're excited to continue to innovate as we open the solution to a wider audience. 

Starting today, you can find AI Observability in Grafana Cloud and start using it right away. Start with the demo mode to get a feel for how it works with some example data. And when you’re ready, you can hook up your own agents and start analyzing them.

For more information on this and all the other exciting updates from GrafanaCON 2026, check out our announcement blog for all the news. And for more information on Grafana Cloud AI, including FAQs about Assistant and our other AI capabilities, check out our AI observability page.

】

可观测性行业已经开发出了出色的工具，用于使用指标、日志、跟踪和配置文件来监控在过去十年的软件开发中占据主导地位的云原生应用程序。 

但是，当谈到理解人工智能系统实际在做什么时，我们常常只能阅读原始对话，猜测质量，并且反应得太晚。这是一个问题。 

代理以传统可观察性无法处理的方式做出决策、调用工具、生成内容以及与用户、服务和应用程序交互。随着组织从云原生转向人工智能原生，越来越明显的是，座席聊天和会话需要与其他更传统的遥测一起被视为一流信号。 

为了解决这一新出现的差距，我们在 Grafana Cloud 中推出了AI 可观测性。 现已推出公开预览版，AI Observability 实际上是作为一个内部黑客马拉松项目开始的，旨在解决我们自己的一些代理挑战。从那时起，我们收到了许多处理类似问题的客户的反馈，因此我们决定将我们所学到的知识转化为一个完整的解决方案，供在生产中运行代理的团队使用，帮助他们了解 AI 正在做什么、做得如何以及哪里出现了问题。

AI 可观测性如何为您提供帮助

传统的可观测性为您提供 CPU 使用情况、请求延迟和错误率等信号。这些很重要。但它们不会告诉您，您的代理是否会随着时间的推移而有所帮助、产生幻觉或悄然退化。

这就是为什么我们在 Grafana Cloud 中构建 AI 可观察性来帮助团队：

  • 实时观察 AI 代理行为，包括输入、输出和执行流
  • 持续评估输出，并对低质量响应、策略违规或异常等问题发出警报行为
  • 尽早发现风险，包括潜在的数据暴露或滥用（例如，泄露的凭据或异常使用模式）
  • 将代理会话和对话提升为一流的遥测信号，并在观察应用程序的同一环境中将它们关联起来

AI Observability 将代理直接连接到跟踪、工具调用、令牌使用、成本和（实时）评估。它在同一个 Grafana Cloud 环境中完成这一切，您可以在其中观察系统的其余部分。 

这为您提供了真正的端到端信号——代理信号或其他信号。因此，下次出现问题时，您将不仅仅看到延迟激增。您还可以打开确切的对话，检查发生了什么，并了解原因。

一次检测，通过开放标准了解一切

AI Observability 与 OpenTelemetry 兼容，因此它自然适合现有的可观察性设置。您对应用程序进行一次检测使用精简 SDK，AI Observability 会自动捕获：

  • 生成和对话
  • 模型和提供者元数据
  • 工具使用
  • 延迟和令牌指标
  • 成本信号

从那里，一切都可以在一个地方查询和探索。您还可以按模型或提供商、时间范围、标签或环境进行过滤。如果您有不同的提供商，同一模型在不同的环境中可能会有不同的功能。

AI Observability 会自动为您分类和编录代理版本，您可以单独检查代理版本，这有助于您找到性能最佳的代理并发现代理可能存在的特定问题。

代理可以巧妙地破坏 AI；可观察性为您提供了解原因的背景

问题通常很微妙，不会出现任何崩溃，但用户会抱怨响应变得越来越长、越来越无用、成本逐渐下降、用户正在失去信任。

AI 可观察性正是针对这个问题而设计的。使用情况和成本细分、分数、评级和注释。

这对于调试您的代理至关重要，或者您还可以了解您的代币的去向，从而了解您的资金去向，或者某些工具是否缓慢或难以执行某些任务，从而增加您的成本。

如果您需要额外的调试支持或建议。有关如何改进代理的信息，您可以使用自然语言向 Grafana Assistant 寻求帮助，因为它可以将您的 AI 数据与所有其他遥测信号相关联，因此可以为各种用例提供帮助。

例如，它可以了解您在计算上花费了多少时间，或者是什么导致了峰值。本质上，它利用了我们现有的全栈可观测性平台的强大功能，并将其扩展到您的企业所依赖的下一代应用程序。

在重要时获取警报

AI 可观测性还可以帮助评估您的 AI 的准确性，当您有多个代理大规模运行时，这可能会成为一项重大挑战。

您可以使用 LLM 作为法官，启发式或正则表达式来检测不需要的输出。而且由于 AI Observability 本身与 Grafana Alerting 集成，因此当您的代理行为异常时，您可以收到通知的控制。总的来说，这使您可以像对待其他服务和基础设施一样对待代理。

您甚至可以将其与 Assistant技能和代理特定的操作手册结合起来。这样，当您收到有关代理的毒性增加的传呼时，您可以要求 Google 助理检查对话、阅读您的操作手册并提供补救策略。

人工智能可观察性，来自构建 Grafana Assistant 的团队

我们了解观察代理的挑战，因为我们已经经历过这一挑战。当我们开始构建 Assistant 时，我们查看了很多框架，但它们对于我们想要如何监控代理而言不够详细。

我们很快意识到我们需要构建自己的内部解决方案。 Google 助理深受用户欢迎，事实上，我们正在以令人兴奋的新方式提供它，而这一成功的一个重要原因是我们能够密切监控我们构建的反馈循环，以便我们能够跟踪性能和客户行为的演变。 

这一成功让我们认为我们应该将其提供给我们的客户。因此，在最近的一次黑客马拉松中，Assistant 团队从监控代理、即时工程、跟踪代理版本、处理工具等方面汲取了经验，并将其融入到一个解决方案中，使您也能够大规模监控代理。 

与 Assistant 一样，AI 可观察性是持续创新的核心。在私人预览期间与客户合作后，我们已经发布了新功能，包括用户注释和简化的警报。我们很高兴能够继续创新，向更广泛的受众开放解决方案。 

从今天开始，您可以在 Grafana Cloud 中找到 AI Observability 并立即开始使用它。从演示模式开始，了解它如何处理一些示例数据。准备好后，您可以连接自己的代理并开始分析它们。

有关此内容以及 GrafanaCON 2026 的所有其他令人兴奋的更新的更多信息，请查看我们的 公告博客了解所有新闻。有关 Grafana Cloud AI 的更多信息，包括有关 Assistant 和我们其他 AI 功能的常见问题解答，请查看我们的 AI 可观测性页面。

item.title: 【Grafana Assistant everywhere: Customize and connect to the AI agent to fit your specific needs】Grafana Assistant 无处不在：自定义并连接到 AI 代理以满足您的特定需求
item.description:
【

The ways you and your teams build and observe your systems are changing. It’s no longer just engineers looking at dashboards, or writing queries or config files. More often, it’s an agent interacting with the data, too, helping write code, run applications, investigate incidents, rightsize deployments, and more. 

At the same time, AI has enabled even faster software development cycles, making it essential that you have access to the right information and the right workflows right when you need them, without having to worry about context switching or hopping between tools.

As a result, we're making big changes to how we think of Grafana Assistant, our purpose-built LLM agent in Grafana Cloud. We're excited to share new ways you and your agents can connect and customize this powerful tool—including accessing Grafana Assistant from self-managed environments—because where and how you run Grafana shouldn’t determine whether you can benefit from Assistant.

Moving Assistant from cloud agent to anywhere agent

As part of the GrafanaCON 2026 keynote, we announced that access to Assistant now extends to Grafana Enterprise and Grafana OSS users. This makes Assistant available in your self-managed environment to help you analyze telemetry data and code in real time, build dashboards, ask questions, and more. 

Self-managed Grafana users can create a Grafana Cloud account and connect it to their Grafana installation via a one-click setup. The assistant is included in the Grafana Cloud forever free plan with generous limits so that you can get started right away. You can also watch the video below to see how easy it is to get started.

Grafana Cloud customers have saved lots of time and effort using Assistant. Working alongside the agent and using natural language, they've used the assistant to help explain concepts, query data, build dashboards, resolve incidents, and even expand adoption.

“There are so many signals and metrics to correlate that it’s a job a computer can do better than humans. With Grafana Assistant, we see fewer incidents, and when they happen the resolution is faster, so the team can stay focused on deploying new features,” said Alessio Paccoia, Head of Technology, Cubbio.io

And now we're excited to extend those same capabilities to our broader community. We still believe Grafana Cloud is the best place to get the full benefits of AI-powered observability, but we also believe in choice and meeting our users where they are. If you're not quite ready to go all-in on cloud, for whatever reason, this can be a great way to try it out and see if it fits your needs. 

How it works

Assistant uses a custom plugin that runs in your browser. Your raw observability data stays in your Grafana instance, and only processed summaries and results are transmitted using our custom tooling architecture. The assistant also "shows its work" by displaying the full conversation history. Any errors or warnings from tool usage are fed back into the conversation, allowing Assistant to iterate and correct mistakes. 

For more information, check out our Assistant docs. You can also get important details on our pricing page, including what's available in our generous free tier.

Customize Assistant for your unique needs

Every organization's observability strategy and workflows are different, so we also want to make sure Assistant can be tailored to your needs. That's why we're excited to make Assistant skills generally available.

Skills are documents you create to guide Assistant agents with instructions, context, and specialized knowledge. They essentially help you encode how your team troubleshoots services, handles specific alerts, and manages shared infrastructure. 

Skills now include a new auto-approve feature you can use to write your runbooks, connect to other tools (e.g., GitHub, Cloudflare, other observability platforms, etc.), and auto-approve tool calls of your choice.

When you pair auto-approve with Assistant Investigations, which helps with multi-step investigations, you can even create your own auto-remediation pipeline that’s triggered from an alert. The result? Pretty much anything you need, from raising a PR in GitHub or GitLab to sending a Slack message to someone to assigning a task in Notion.

Stay on top of everything with automations

Observability teams have a lot to keep track of, and that's only increasing now that agents are becoming central to software development. To help you stay on top of everything, we're introducing Assistant automations, which you can use to get automatic summaries of what's happening in your environment.

By pairing automations with skills, you can trigger Assistant to handle any task at any time, with or without you. Want a daily report of all alerts that fired yesterday? A rundown of incidents that were resolved last week? The error rate in your product catalog or whether the latest deployments changed p99? Simply connect to our available integrations or any API, write a skill, and you can get full analyses about these or any other questions you have about your stack and its performance. 

Say 'hello' to Assistant outside of Grafana Cloud

You have your own way of interacting with your systems and your teammates, and odds are that's not done entirely through Grafana Cloud. We want to meet you where you are, so we're expanding the ways you can access Assistant, whether that's through Slack, Microsoft Teams, an API, or the CLI. 

For example, you can now build automations with the Assistant CLI, chat with colleagues and the Assistant in Slack, have Claude Code or Codex collaborate with the Assistant via the CLI, or make requests from a remote machine to the Assistant.

This is all about finding new ways to integrate Assistant into your workflows, rather than forcing you into our UI. Make Assistant work the way that works for you and stop getting slowed down by constant context switching.

Bring your own agent: remote hosted MCP server and our new gcx CLI tool

In addition to bringing Assistant to you, you can now bring more to Assistant. With our new remote hosted MCP server and the new gcx CLI tool, your agents can talk to Assistant, Grafana Cloud, or both.

Use the remote hosted MCP server to connect any agent to the same sophisticated tools that Assistant uses in Grafana Cloud. You don't need to install any dependencies; just point your agent at mcp.grafana.com/mcp to get access to your metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, alerts, incidents, and more. This also allows you to connect other cloud agents to Grafana Cloud if you want to build your own assistant.

If you prefer a local-first approach, gcx unifies grafanactl and the Assistant CLI with agent-first, new CLI tools to your command line and your agentic coding environment. It connects your editor to your entire production stack so your agent can write code that's observability-aware from the start. Instrument a new service, investigate a firing alert, or draft a fix informed by real production data—all without leaving your editor.

Connect any API with Assistant

And don't forget that Assistant can use the Infinity data source to send any GET or POST requests to any publicly or privately available API endpoint. This recent upgrade makes Assistant the center of your DevOps lifecycle, helping you connect it to any other tool, correlate any data, and remediate faster than ever. Or use the capability to check if you caught all Pokémon yet.

More ways to take your Assistant experience to the next level

We're excited for you to try these new features that expand the reach of Assistant, but this isn't everything. We're constantly looking for new ways to improve Assistant to help you improve your observability practices. Here are just some of the other updates we recently released: 

  • The new workspace view, which brings Assistant into full-page mode so you can browse your chats and supporting data in one view
  • Learn mode, which helps you quickly get up to speed on Grafana Assistant with tutorials and example tasks that are personalized to your stack 
  • Reworked context management and infrastructure memories for your stack help so Assistant knows where to look from the start 
  • EU inference for our European customers
  • A Python runtime for Assistant to process large amounts of data
  • Integrations with 15 other Grafana Cloud services or features and more than 50 third-party data sources

For more information on this and all the other exciting updates from GrafanaCON 2026, check out our announcement blog for all the news. And for more information on Grafana Cloud AI, including FAQs about Assistant and our other AI capabilities, check out our AI observability page.

】

您和您的团队构建和观察系统的方式正在发生变化。工程师不再只是查看仪表板、编写查询或配置文件。更常见的是，它也是与数据交互的代理，帮助编写代码、运行应用程序、调查事件、调整部署规模等。 

同时，人工智能实现了更快的软件开发周期，因此您必须在需要时访问正确的信息和正确的工作流程，而不必担心上下文切换或工具之间的切换。

因此，我们正在对 Grafana Assistant（我们的 Grafana Assistant）的看法做出重大改变。 Grafana Cloud 中专门构建的 LLM 代理。我们很高兴与您和您的代理分享连接和自定义此强大工具的新方法（包括从自我管理环境访问 Grafana Assistant），因为运行 Grafana 的位置和方式不应决定您是否可以从 Assistant 中受益。

将 Assistant 从云代理移动到任何代理

作为 GrafanaCON 的一部分2026 年主题演讲中，我们宣布对 Assistant 的访问权限现在扩展到 Grafana Enterprise 和 Grafana OSS 用户。这使得 Assistant 在您的自我管理环境中可用，帮助您实时分析遥测数据和代码、构建仪表板、提出问题等。 

自我管理的 Grafana 用户可以创建一个 Grafana Cloud 帐户，并通过一键设置将其连接到 Grafana 安装。该助手包含在 Grafana Cloud 永久免费计划中，具有慷慨的限制，以便您可以立即开始。您还可以观看下面的视频，了解入门是多么容易。

Grafana Cloud 客户使用 Assistant 节省了大量时间和精力。他们与代理一起工作并使用自然语言，使用助手来帮助解释概念、查询数据、构建仪表板、解决事件，甚至扩大采用范围。

“有如此多的信号和指标相互关联，这是一项计算机可以比人类做得更好的工作。有了 Grafana Assistant，我们看到的事件更少，而且当事件发生时解决速度更快，因此团队可以专注于部署新功能，”Cubbio.io

现在，我们很高兴能够将这些相同的功能扩展到我们更广泛的社区。我们仍然相信 Grafana Cloud 是充分发挥 AI 支持的可观察性优势的最佳场所，但我们也相信选择并满足用户的需求。如果您还没有准备好全面使用云，无论出于何种原因，这可能是一个很好的尝试方式，看看它是否适合您的需求。 

工作原理

Assistant 使用在浏览器中运行的自定义插件。您的原始可观测性数据保留在 Grafana 实例中，并且仅使用我们的自定义工具架构传输经过处理的摘要和结果。该助手还通过显示完整的对话历史记录来“展示其工作”。工具使用中的任何错误或警告都会反馈到对话中，从而允许助手迭代并纠正错误。 

有关更多信息，请查看我们的助理文档。您还可以在我们的定价页面上获取重要详细信息，包括我们慷慨的免费套餐中提供的内容。

根据您的独特需求自定义 Google 助理

每个组织的可观察性策略和工作流程都不同，因此我们还希望确保 Google 助理能够根据您的需求进行定制。因此，我们很高兴能够普遍提供助理技能。

技能是您创建的文档，用于为助理客服人员提供说明、背景信息和专业知识。它们本质上是帮助您编码团队服务故障排除、处理特定警报和管理共享基础设施的方式。 

技能现在包括新的自动批准功能，您可以使用它来编写运行手册、连接到其他工具（例如 GitHub、Cloudflare、其他可观察平台等）以及自动批准您选择的工具调用。

当您将自动批准与 助理调查，有助于进行多步骤调查，您甚至可以创建自己的由警报触发的自动修复管道。结果呢？几乎有您需要的任何东西，从在 GitHub 或 GitLab 中提出 PR，到向某人发送 Slack 消息，再到在 Notion 中分配任务。

通过自动化掌控一切

可观察性团队有很多事情需要跟踪，而且随着代理成为软件开发的核心，这种情况只会越来越多。为了帮助您掌握一切，我们引入了 Google 助理自动化功能，您可以使用它来自动总结您的环境中发生的情况。

通过将自动化功能与技能配对，您可以随时触发 Google 助理处理任何任务，无论您是否在场。想要一份昨天发出的所有警报的每日报告吗？上周解决的事件的概要？您的产品目录中的错误率或最新部署是否更改了 p99？只需连接到我们可用的集成或任何 API，编写一项技能，您就可以获得有关这些或任何 API 的完整分析您对堆栈及其性能有任何其他疑问。 

在 Grafana Cloud 之外向 Assistant 打招呼

您有自己的方式与系统和队友交互，但很可能这并不完全通过 Grafana Cloud 完成。我们希望在您所在的地方与您见面，因此我们正在扩展您访问 Assistant 的方式，无论是通过 Slack、Microsoft Teams、API 还是 CLI。 

例如，您现在可以使用 Assistant CLI 构建自动化，在 Slack 中与同事和 Assistant 聊天，让 Claude Code 或 Codex 通过 CLI 与 Assistant 协作，或者从远程计算机向 Assistant 发出请求。

这一切都是为了寻找将 Assistant 集成到您的工作流程中的新方法，而不是强迫您使用我们的 UI。让 Assistant 以适合您的方式工作，不再因不断的上下文切换而减慢速度。

自带代理：远程托管的 MCP 服务器和我们新的 gcx CLI 工具

除了为您带来 Assistant 之外，您现在还可以为 Assistant 带来更多功能。借助我们新的远程托管 MCP 服务器和新的 gcx CLI 工具，您的代理可以与 Assistant、Grafana Cloud 或两者通信。

使用远程托管的 MCP 服务器将任何代理连接到 Assistant 在 Grafana Cloud 中使用的相同复杂工具。您不需要安装任何依赖项；只需将您的代理指向 mcp.grafana.com/mcp 即可访问您的指标、日志、跟踪、仪表板、警报、事件等。如果您想构建自己的助手，这还允许您将其他云代理连接到 Grafana Cloud。

如果您更喜欢本地优先的方法，gcx 会统一 grafanactl 和 Assistant CLI将代理优先的新 CLI 工具添加到您的命令行和代理编码环境中。它将您的编辑器连接到整个生产堆栈，以便您的代理可以从一开始就编写具有可观察性的代码。检测新服务、调查触发警报或起草由实际生产数据通知的修复程序 - 所有这些都无需离开编辑器。

将任何 API 与 Assistant 连接

并且不要忘记，Assistant 可以使用 Infinity 数据源将任何 GET 或 POST 请求发送到任何公开或私有的 API 端点。最近的升级使 Assistant 成为 DevOps 生命周期的中心，帮助您将其连接到任何其他工具、关联任何数据并比以往更快地进行修复。或者使用该功能检查您是否捕捉到了所有神奇宝贝。

更多方式将您的 Google 助理体验提升到新水平级别

我们很高兴您能够尝试这些扩展 r 的新功能每个 Assistant，但这还不是全部。我们不断寻找改进 Google 助理的新方法，以帮助您改进可观察性实践。以下是我们最近发布的一些其他更新：

  • 新的工作区视图，使 Google 助理进入全页模式，以便您可以在一个视图中浏览聊天和支持数据
  • 学习模式，通过针对您的堆栈进行个性化的教程和示例任务，帮助您快速掌握 Grafana Assistant 的速度
  • 重新设计了堆栈帮助的上下文管理和基础架构内存，以便 Google 助理知道从何处查看
  • 为我们的欧洲客户启动 EU 推理
  • 用于 Assistant 处理大量数据的 Python 运行时
  • 与其他 15 种 Grafana Cloud 服务或功能以及 50 多个第三方数据源集成

有关此内容以及 GrafanaCON 2026 中所有其他令人兴奋的更新的更多信息，请查看我们的 公告博客了解所有新闻。有关 Grafana Cloud AI 的更多信息，包括有关 Assistant 和我们其他 AI 功能的常见问题解答，请查看我们的 AI 可观测性页面。

item.title: 【Grafana 13 release: get value from your data faster, manage operations at scale, and more!】Grafana 13 版本：更快地从数据中获取价值、大规模管理运营等等！
item.description:
【

Who says 13 is unlucky? With the release of Grafana 13, we're giving the community the most streamlined, flexible, and intuitive Grafana experience yet.

Unveiled during the opening keynote of GrafanaCON 2026, the latest major release is all about helping you get value from your data faster, whether you’re spinning up dashboards, operating Grafana at scale, or extending the platform as your requirements change. 

For those of you who couldn’t join us in Barcelona for GrafanaCON, you can explore some Grafana 13 highlights below and check out the Grafana 13 deep dive session when it's available on demand. (And if you want to read all the headlines from our biggest community event of the year, check out our GrafanaCON announcements blog.)

For a complete list of Grafana 13 updates, refer to our Grafana documentation, our What’s New documentation, and the Grafana changelog, and be sure to watch the TL;DR video below.

Note: If you upgraded to Grafana 13 prior to April 16, please see an important note about Git Sync in our docs.

Get meaningful insights from data faster

For many teams, the challenge isn’t collecting metrics—it’s turning that data into clear, actionable insights on demand. Too often, valuable data exists, but the path to a useful dashboard is slow or uncertain. 

That’s why with Grafana 13 we’ve been focused on helping you move from a blank canvas to meaningful visualizations and insights in just a few clicks. Here's how:

Use pre-built dashboards based on your data 

With suggested dashboards in Grafana, you can access pre-built dashboards that are tailored to your specific data sources. In Grafana 13, we’ve made significant improvements to make these dashboards even easier to find, evaluate, and use.

Grafana will automatically surface suggested dashboards for you if there is an existing dashboard available for your data source. (Instead of the Build a dashboard button, you will be able to choose a From suggestions option.)

From there, you will see a curated list of provisioned dashboards that Grafana Labs will maintain for supported data sources alongside community dashboards you can reference, with built-in search capabilities to help you find specific dashboards quickly.

For Prometheus users, Grafana goes one step further by evaluating how well a suggested dashboard matches the exact metrics available in your data source. Each dashboard is assigned a compatibility score, helping you quickly understand how much customization may be required before it becomes useful.

Suggested dashboards are available in public preview in all editions of Grafana. To learn more about these and other updates, please visit our docs. 

Discover smarter ways to represent your data 

Visualization suggestions help you find and compare different dashboard panels and pinpoint the best way to showcase your data for your use case and end users. 

In Grafana 13, we've refined the suggestions for visualization types so that they are not only based on the data surfaced by queries in a panel. They now leverage metadata from your data sources, such as data frame types in the data plane, to recommend specific visualization types best suited to your data.

Even better? When you preview a visualization using the Suggestions feature, you'll get a full-sized preview of the targeted suggestion in the panel editor before you select it. 

Visualization suggestions are now generally available in all editions of Grafana. 

Cut down on repetitive query work

Writing queries is one of Grafana’s most powerful features, but also one of the most tedious. With saved queries, teams can save, share, and reuse their queries, reducing duplicate work, speeding up onboarding, and ensuring everyone builds on proven best practices. 

Here are the latest updates. 

New interface and improved filtering experience

We’ve refreshed the saved queries interface to make discovery and management easier with a new dialog box, improved organization, and persistent filters that remember your preferences.

Faster panel creation

You can now create panels directly from saved queries. Grafana will automatically generate a panel with a recommended visualization, without having to go into panel edit mode. This helps you move from query to dashboard faster.

More flexibility with variables

You can now substitute variables when selecting a saved query without modifying the original query. This makes saved queries more portable and reusable in environments where variable names or available values differ between dashboards.

These new features are currently available in public preview in Grafana Cloud and Grafana Enterprise. To learn more, please visit our docs. 

Build complex queries faster

We’ve redesigned the query editor to make complex panels easier to build and manage. The new experience gives you a unified view of your entire data pipeline, including queries, expressions, transformations, and alerts, with clearer feedback and faster access to key settings.

Updates include:

  • Streamlined pipeline management: A new sidebar shows queries, expressions, and transformations as color-coded cards with state indicators (error, hidden, disabled). 
  • Per-query data source selection: Each query now has its own data source picker, making multi-source panels more intuitive. 
  • Inline alert visibility: A dedicated Alerts tab shows all alert rules tied to the panel, with color-coded status so you can quickly assess alert health.
  • Improved query options access: A footer surfaces key panel-level options like max data points and time overrides, with inline indicators to highlight deviations from defaults.

The redesigned query editor is available in private preview in all editions of Grafana.  

Build dashboards, analyze data, and more with AI

A big announcement to come out of the GrafanaCON 2026 opening keynote: Grafana OSS and Grafana Enterprise users can now access Grafana Assistant, our AI-powered agent in Grafana Cloud, as an additional tool to quickly and easily gain insights to your data. With the help of Grafana Assistant, you can customize suggested dashboards and templates, streamline your use of SQL expressions, and so much more.

To learn more, please read our Assistant everywhere blog post. 

New and improved data visualizations

It wouldn’t be a Grafana release without some new ways to visualize your data. Here are a few data visualization highlights in Grafana 13.

Revamped gauge panel 

The updated gauge panel is now generally available in all editions of Grafana, offering a range of new data representation options based on your needs.

You can learn more about the gauge visualization in our docs. 

Graphviz panel to visualize dynamic workflows

The new Graphviz panel plugin lets you render diagrams defined in the Graphviz DOT language mapped to live data from any data source. 

You can now map metrics to service topologies, infrastructure dependencies, business workflows, or any structure where relationships matter as much as values. This helps you understand at which point a process or flow is failing, and act fast.

The Graphviz panel plugin is available in private preview for all editions of Grafana. 

Faster configuration with panel styles

You can now apply panel styles to quickly update your panels without adjusting multiple options manually. 

Panel styles gives you a curated set of configurations, such as colors, thresholds, and display options, so you can get to a more polished visualization with a single click.

Panel styles are currently supported in time series, gauge, bar gauge, stat, and bar chart visualizations, and are in public preview in all editions of Grafana.

Copy-paste panel styles 

Styling panels in Grafana used to require a lot of manual work: editing each panel individually, or duplicating one just to reuse its look and then swapping out the query. 

With the new copy-and-paste feature, you can copy the styling from one panel and apply it to another in just a couple of clicks.

This feature is available for core panels, and you can only copy styles between panels of the same type (for example, stat to stat or gauge to gauge). It’s currently experimental in all editions of Grafana, and you can enable it with the panelStyleActions feature toggle.

Navigate and streamline your data with dynamic dashboards

As teams add more visualizations and connect more data sources to Grafana, dashboards quickly become larger and more complex. This can lead to cluttered layouts, empty panels, and excessive scrolling, making dashboards harder to navigate and maintain.

Enter dynamic dashboards, a dashboarding experience that’s more intuitive, responsive, and scalable to support growing teams.

"With dynamic dashboards, it feels like the limits do not exist anymore,” said Dawid Dębowski, Software Engineer at G2A.COM. “You can make the dashboards fully dynamic by hiding the parts that are not needed based on variables or queries, and you can better organize the dashboard thanks to awesome tabs. The possibilities seem endless."

Dynamic dashboards is now on by default for all Grafana Cloud users and in self-managed Grafana instances. Every new and existing dashboard uses the new layout engine, editing experience, and dashboard structure automatically. Your existing dashboards are migrated to the new schema (more on this below) when you open them. No manual steps required.

Since public preview earlier this year, we’ve specifically focused on refining the editing experience within dynamic dashboards. Updates include:

  • New dashboard creation page with a default layout selector, so you start with the right layout from the beginning.
  • Variables and annotations in the sidebar with drag-and-drop reordering, inline editing, and color indicators for annotations.
  • Drag-and-drop panel creation from the sidebar directly onto the canvas.
  • Deeper nesting up to three levels, so you can place rows inside tabs inside rows.
  • Paste panels using the new Add mode of the sidebar.
  • Sidebar docks in edit mode and floats in view mode. The sidebar defaults to docked when editing and undocked when viewing, so it takes up space only when you need it. Your preference is remembered per mode.

What this means for provisioned dashboards: a new dashboard schema 

Dynamic dashboards uses a new dashboard schema (v2). Dashboards provisioned as code (using the API, Terraform, or Git Sync) continue to work. V1 dashboards are migrated to v2 automatically when loaded. 

For details on the v2 schema and provisioning, refer to the dashboard schema documentation.

And to learn more about dynamic dashboards, refer to the dynamic dashboards documentation.

Operate Grafana at scale 

Grafana 13 is here to make Grafana easier to configure and maintain at scale. The goal is simple: help admins spend less time firefighting and more time enabling their organization, with safer changes, lower operational risk, and better support for running Grafana across teams and environments. 

Manage observability as code: the latest in Git Sync 

As your Grafana instance scales, managing dozens (or even hundreds) of dashboards through the UI can quickly become overwhelming. Tracking changes gets murky, dashboards multiply, and consistency suffers.

Git Sync addresses this challenge by bringing a native GitOps workflow into your Grafana instance. This enables teams to version dashboards, submit pull requests, and manage approvals seamlessly.

Git Sync is now generally available in all editions of Grafana, and includes new features that give you more flexibility and control when managing dashboards as code, including GitHub App authentication and support for GitLab, BitBucket, and pure Git. 

To learn more about Git Sync, check out our documentation.

Keep instances healthy with Grafana Advisor 

Grafana Advisor, now generally available in all editions of Grafana, helps you keep your Grafana instance running smoothly and securely. It automatically performs regular health checks on your Grafana server, providing actionable insights and recommendations for maintaining optimal system performance.

Admins can use Advisor to uncover issues, such as:

  • Data sources with a failing health check, which would result in failed queries
  • Outdated plugins that may be insecure or missing the latest fixes and features
  • Misconfigured SSO settings

The Advisor also integrates with Grafana Assistant, giving you AI-powered guidance to quickly remediate identified issues. 

For more information, refer to our documentation.

Extend Grafana through an open ecosystem 

In line with our big tent philosophy, we believe you should be able to query and visualize your data, regardless of where your data lives. As a result, we’re always working to expand and enhance our line-up of Grafana data sources. 

New Enterprise data source: IBM DB2

For many organizations, IBM DB2 is the database powering their most critical systems. Now you can bring DB2 data directly into Grafana and visualize it alongside the rest of your telemetry.

The new IBM DB2 Enterprise data source, now in public preview, lets you connect to your DB2 databases and run SQL queries right within Grafana Cloud or Grafana Enterprise. 

You can:

  • Write full SQL queries with syntax highlighting in the query editor.
  • Format results as tables for detailed records or as time-series for trend charts.
  • Use template variables to make dashboards dynamic and reusable.
  • Surface annotations to mark events or milestones on your graphs.
  • Drive Grafana alerting directly from DB2 query results

Whether you’re analyzing business records or tracking operational performance, you have full control over how your DB2 data is explored and visualized.

The IBM DB2 Enterprise data source is one of several Enterprise data sources we’ve recently added to our line-up, alongside SolarWinds and Jenkins. 

Introducing the Grafana Marketplace

The Grafana Marketplace, also announced at GrafanaCON 2026, is a new platform that allows independent software vendors (ISVs), systems integrators (SIs), and developers in the Grafana community to sell and distribute plugins developed for Grafana. 

The marketplace is currently in its pilot phase. To learn more, including how to get involved, please see our marketplace blog post. 

Learn more about Grafana 13

For an in-depth list of all the new features in Grafana, check out our Grafana documentation, the Grafana changelog, or our What’s New documentation.

Join the Grafana Labs community

We invite you to engage with the Grafana Labs community forums. Share your experiences with the new features, discuss best practices, and explore creative ways to integrate these updates into your workflows. Your insights and use cases are invaluable in enriching the Grafana ecosystem.

Upgrade to Grafana 13

Download Grafana 13 today or experience all the latest features by signing up for Grafana Cloud, which offers an actually useful forever-free tier and plans for every use case. Sign up for a free Grafana Cloud account today.

Our Grafana upgrade guide also provides step-by-step instructions for those looking to upgrade from an earlier version to ensure a smooth transition.

A special thanks to our community

We extend our heartfelt gratitude to the Grafana community!

Your contributions, ranging from pull requests to valuable feedback, are crucial in continually enhancing Grafana. And your enthusiasm and dedication inspire us at Grafana Labs to persistently innovate and elevate the Grafana platform.

For more information on this and all the other exciting updates from GrafanaCON 2026, check out our announcements blog post.

】

谁说13不吉利？随着 Grafana 13 的发布，我们将为社区提供迄今为止最精简、灵活、直观的 Grafana 体验。

在 GrafanaCON 2026 的开幕主题演讲中发布的最新主要版本旨在帮助您更快地从数据中获取价值，无论您是在启动仪表板、操作大规模 Grafana，或根据您的需求变化扩展平台。 

对于那些无法参加我们在巴塞罗那参加 GrafanaCON 的人，您可以探索下面的一些 Grafana 13 亮点，并在按需提供时查看 Grafana 13 深入探讨会议。 （如果您想阅读我们今年最大的社区活动的所有头条新闻，请查看我们的GrafanaCON 公告博客。）

有关 Grafana 13 更新的完整列表，请参阅我们的Grafana 文档、我们的新增功能文档以及Grafana 变更日志，并务必观看下面的 TL;DR 视频。

注意：如果您在 4 月 16 日之前升级到 Grafana 13，请参阅 关于 Git Sync 的重要说明在我们的文档中。

更快地从数据中获取有意义的见解

对于许多团队来说，挑战不是收集指标，而是根据需要将数据转化为清晰、可操作的见解。很多时候，有价值的数据是存在的，但通往有用仪表板的道路却很缓慢或不确定。 

这就是为什么我们在 Grafana 13 中致力于帮助您只需点击几下鼠标即可从空白画布转变为有意义的可视化和见解。操作方法如下：

根据您的数据使用预构建的仪表板

借助 Grafana 中的建议的仪表板，您可以访问预构建的仪表板根据您的特定数据源定制的仪表板。在 Grafana 13 中，我们进行了重大改进，使这些仪表板更易于查找、评估和使用。

Grafana 将自动为您显示建议的仪表板（如果有）是可用于您的数据源的现有仪表板。 （您可以选择来自建议选项，而不是构建仪表板按钮。）

Fro在那里，您将看到 Grafana Labs 将为支持的数据源维护的精选仪表板列表以及您可以参考的社区仪表板，并具有内置搜索功能，可帮助您快速找到特定的仪表板。

对于 Prometheus 用户，Grafana 更进一步，评估建议的仪表板与数据源中可用的确切指标的匹配程度。每个仪表板都会分配一个兼容性分数，帮助您快速了解在其发挥作用之前可能需要多少自定义程度。

建议的仪表板可在所有版本的 Grafana 中以公共预览版形式提供。要了解有关这些更新和其他更新的更多信息，请访问我们的文档。 

发现更智能的数据表示方式

可视化建议可帮助您查找和比较不同的仪表板面板，并确定为您的用例和最终用户展示数据的最佳方式。 

在 Grafana 13 中，我们细化了可视化类型的建议，以便它们不仅仅基于面板中查询显示的数据。他们现在利用数据源中的元数据（例如数据平面中的数据框架类型）来推荐最适合您的数据的特定可视化类型。

甚至更好？当您使用建议功能预览可视化效果时，在选择目标建议之前，您将在面板编辑器中获得目标建议的全尺寸预览。 

可视化建议现已在所有版本的 Grafana 中普遍提供。 

减少重复查询工作

编写查询是 Grafana 最强大的功能之一，但也是最乏味的功能之一。通过保存的查询，团队可以保存、共享和重用他们的查询，减少重复工作，加快入门速度，并确保每个人都建立在经过验证的最佳实践之上。 

以下是最新更新。 

新界面和改进的过滤体验

我们刷新了已保存的查询界面，通过新的对话框、改进的组织和持久过滤器使发现和管理变得更容易记住您的偏好设置。

更快地创建面板

您现在可以直接从保存的查询创建面板。 Grafana 将自动生成一个带有推荐的面板可视化，无需进入面板编辑模式。这有助于您更快地从查询转移到仪表板。

使用变量更加灵活

您现在可以在选择已保存的查询时替换变量，而无需修改原始查询。这使得保存的查询在仪表板之间变量名称或可用值不同的环境中更加便携和可重用。

这些新功能目前在 Grafana Cloud 和 Grafana Enterprise 中提供公共预览版。要了解更多信息，请访问我们的文档。 

更快地构建复杂查询

我们重新设计了查询编辑器，使复杂面板更易于构建和管理。新体验为您提供整个数据管道（包括查询、表达式、转换和警报）的统一视图，并提供更清晰的反馈和更快地访问关键设置。

更新包括：

  • 简化的管道管理：新的侧边栏将查询、表达式和转换显示为带有状态指示器（错误、隐藏、禁用）的颜色编码卡。 
  • 每个查询的数据源选择：每个查询现在都有自己的数据源选择器，使多源面板更加直观。 
  • 内联警报可见性：专用的警报选项卡显示与面板相关的所有警报规则，并以颜色编码状态，以便您快速评估警报运行状况。
  • 改进的查询选项访问：页脚显示关键面板级选项，例如最大数据点和时间覆盖，并使用内联指示器突出显示与默认值的偏差。

重新设计的查询编辑器在所有版本的 Grafana 中都提供私人预览版。  

利用 AI 构建仪表板、分析数据等

GrafanaCON 2026 开幕主题演讲中发布了一项重大声明：Grafana OSS 和 Grafana Enterprise 用户现在可以访问 Grafana Assistant，这是我们在 Grafana Cloud 中由 AI 驱动的代理，作为快速获取信息的附加工具并轻松洞察您的数据。借助 Grafana Assistant，您可以自定义建议的仪表板和模板、简化 SQL 的使用表达式，等等。

了解更多信息，请阅读我们的助理无处不在博客文章。 

新的和改进的数据可视化

如果没有一些新的数据可视化方法，就不会是 Grafana 版本。以下是 Grafana 13 中的一些数据可视化亮点。

改进的仪表板

更新的仪表板现已在 Grafana 的所有版本中普遍提供，提供了一系列根据您的需求提供新的数据表示选项。

您可以在我们的文档中了解有关仪表可视化的更多信息。 

用于可视化动态工作流程的 Graphviz 面板

新的 Graphviz 面板插件可让您渲染Graphviz 中定义的图表DOT 语言映射到来自任何数据源的实时数据。 

您现在可以将指标映射到服务拓扑、基础设施依赖项、业务工作流程或关系与价值同等重要的任何结构。这可以帮助您了解流程或流程在哪个点出现故障，并快速采取行动。

Graphviz 面板插件在所有版本的 Grafana 中都提供私人预览版。 

使用面板样式进行更快的配置

您现在可以应用面板样式来快速更新面板，而无需手动调整多个选项。 

面板样式为您提供了一组精选的配置，例如颜色、阈值和显示选项，因此您只需单击一下即可获得更精美的可视化效果。

面板样式目前在时间序列、仪表、条形图、统计数据和条形图可视化中受支持，并且在所有版本的 Grafana 中都提供公共预览版。

复制粘贴面板样式

在Grafana 过去需要大量的手动工作：单独编辑每个面板，或者复制一个面板只是为了重用其外观，然后交换查询。 

借助新的复制粘贴功能，您只需单击几下即可从一个面板复制样式并将其应用到另一个面板。

此功能适用于核心面板，您只能在相同类型的面板之间复制样式（例如，统计数据到统计数据或计量表到计量表）。目前，它在 Grafana 的所有版本中都处于实验阶段，您可以通过 panelStyleActions 功能切换来启用它。

使用动态仪表板导航和简化数据

随着团队添加更多可视化并将更多数据源连接到 Grafana，仪表板会快速更新kly 变得更大、更复杂。这可能会导致混乱的布局、空面板和过度滚动，使仪表板更难导航和维护。

使用动态仪表板，这是一种更直观、响应更快且可扩展的仪表板体验，可支持不断增长的业务

“有了动态仪表板，感觉限制不再存在了，”G2A.COM 的软件工程师 Dawid Dębowski 说道。 “您可以通过根据变量或查询隐藏不需要的部分，使仪表板完全动态化，并且借助出色的选项卡，您可以更好地组织仪表板。可能性似乎无穷无尽。”

动态仪表板现在默认为所有 Grafana Cloud 用户和自我管理的 Grafana 实例启用。每个新的和现有的仪表板都会自动使用新的布局引擎、编辑体验和仪表板结构。当您打开现有仪表板时，它们会迁移到新架构（更多信息见下文）。无需手动步骤。

自今年早些时候的公开预览版以来，我们特别致力于改进动态仪表板中的编辑体验。更新包括：

  • 新的仪表板创建页面，带有默认布局选择器，因此您可以从头开始使用正确的布局。
  • 侧栏中的变量和注释，具有拖放重新排序、内联编辑和注释颜色指示符。
  • 将面板创建从侧边栏直接拖放到画布。
  • 更深的嵌套最多三层，因此您可以将行放置在行内的选项卡中。
  • 使用侧边栏的新添加模式粘贴面板。
  • 侧边栏在编辑模式下停靠，在查看模式下浮动。侧边栏默认在编辑时停靠，在查看时取消停靠，因此仅在您需要时才占用空间。每种模式都会记住您的偏好设置。

这对于配置的仪表板意味着什么：新的仪表板架构

动态仪表板使用新的仪表板架构 (v2)。作为代码配置的仪表板（使用 API、Terraform 或 Git Sync）可以继续工作。 V1 仪表板在加载时会自动迁移到 v2。 

有关 v2 架构和配置的详细信息，请参阅仪表板架构文档。

要了解有关动态仪表板的更多信息，请参阅动态仪表板文档。

大规模运营 Grafana

Grafana 13 旨在让 Grafana 更易于大规模配置和维护。目标很简单：帮助管理员花更少的时间在救火上，将更多的时间用于支持他们的组织，并通过更安全的更改、更低的运营风险和更好地支持跨团队和环境运行 Grafana。

以代码形式管理可观察性：Git Sync 的最新功能

随着 Grafana 实例的扩展，通过 UI 管理数十个（甚至数百个）仪表板很快就会变得难以应付，仪表板倍增，一致性受到影响。

Git Sync 通过将本机 GitOps 工作流程引入 Grafana 实例来解决这一挑战。仪表板、提交拉取请求并无缝管理审批。

Git Sync 现已在 Grafana 的所有版本中普遍提供，并包含新功能，让您在以代码方式管理仪表板时具有更大的灵活性和控制力，包括 GitHub 应用程序身份验证以及对 GitLab、BitBucket 和纯 Git 的支持。

要了解有关 Git Sync 的更多信息，请查看我们的 文档。

使用 Grafana Advisor 保持实例健康

Grafana Advisor，现在在所有版本的 Grafana 中普遍可用，帮助您保持 Grafana 实例平稳、安全地运行。它会自动对您的实例执行定期运行状况检查。 Grafana 服务器，提供可操作的见解和建议，以维持最佳系统性能。

管理员可以使用 Advisor 发现问题，例如：

  • 数据源运行状况检查失败，这将导致查询失败
  • 过时的插件可能不安全或缺少最新修复和功能
  • 配置错误的 SSO 设置

Advisor 还与 Grafana Assistant 集成，为您提供由 AI 支持的指导，可快速修复已发现的问题。

有关详细信息，请参阅我们的文档。

通过开放的生态系统扩展 Grafana

根据我们的大帐篷理念，我们相信您应该能够查询和可视化您的数据，无论您的数据位于何处。因此，我们一直致力于扩展和增强 Grafana 数据源阵容。

新的企业数据源：IBM DB2

对于许多组织来说，IBM DB2 是为其最关键系统提供支持的数据库。现在，您可以将 DB2 数据直接引入到其中。Grafana 并将其与遥测的其余部分一起可视化。

新的 IBM DB2 Enterprise 数据源现已提供公共预览版，可让您连接到 DB2 数据库并直接在 Grafana Cloud 或 Grafana Enterprise 中运行 SQL 查询。 

您可以：

  • 在查询编辑器中编写带有语法突出显示的完整 SQL 查询。
  • 将结果格式化为详细记录的表格或趋势图的时间序列。
  • 使用模板变量使仪表板动态且可重用。
  • 表面注释来标记图表上的事件或里程碑。
  • 直接从 DB2 查询驱动 Grafana 警报结果

无论您是分析业务记录还是跟踪运营绩效，您都可以完全控制 DB2 数据的探索和可视化方式。

IBM DB2 Enterprise 数据源是我们最近添加到产品阵容中的多个企业数据源之一，其他数据源还有SolarWinds 和 Jenkins。 

Grafana Marketplace 简介

Grafana Marketplace 也在 GrafanaCON 2026 上宣布，它是一个新平台，允许独立软件供应商 (ISV)、系统集成商 (SI) 和 Grafana 社区的开发人员销售和分发为 Grafana 开发的插件。 

该市场目前正处于试点阶段。要了解更多信息，包括如何参与，请参阅我们的市场博客文章。 

了解有关 Grafana 13 的更多信息

有关 Grafana 中所有新功能的详细列表，请查看我们的 Grafana 文档，Grafana 变更日志，或我们的新增文档。

加入 Grafana 实验室社区

我们邀请您参与Grafana Labs 社区论坛。分享您使用新功能的体验，讨论最佳实践，并探索将这些更新集成到您的工作流程中的创造性方法。您的见解和用例对于丰富 Grafana 生态系统非常宝贵。

升级到 Grafana 13

立即下载Grafana 13，或者通过注册 Grafana Cloud 体验所有最新功能，Grafana Cloud 提供了真正有用的永久免费套餐和适合每次使用的计划案例。注册免费 Grafana Cloud立即帐户。

我们的Grafana 升级指南还为那些希望从早期版本升级的用户提供分步说明，以确保平稳过渡。

特别感谢我们的社区

我们向 Grafana 社区！

您的贡献（从拉取请求到有价值的反馈）对于不断增强 Grafana 至关重要。你们的热情和奉献精神激励着我们 Grafana Labs 不断创新和提升 Grafana 平台。

有关本次更新以及 GrafanaCON 2026 的所有其他令人兴奋的更新的更多信息，请查看我们的公告博客发布。

item.title: 【Grafana Labs acquires Logline to accelerate needle-in-the-haystack log queries】Grafana Labs 收购 Logline 以加速大海捞针日志查询
item.description:
【

From the beginning, Loki has been designed around a simple goal: make log management cost-efficient and easier to operate at scale. That focus is why teams continue to turn to the open source, horizontally scalable, and highly available log aggregation system, which also powers Grafana Cloud Logs, time and time again. 

Loki is powered by a label-based indexing approach that keeps storage costs low and operations lightweight. For the majority of use cases, this design works extremely well. But at large scale, searches involving highly unique values, like request or job IDs, can become more challenging, with queries taking longer to return results. 

As our users push into larger datasets and more demanding use cases, we want to expand what Loki can do, without compromising the cost and simplicity that make it so effective.

That’s why, today at GrafanaCON 2026, we’re excited to share that Grafana Labs has acquired Logline, a company specializing in the execution of “needle–in-the-haystack” queries and full text search. 

Logline was founded by Jason Nochlin, an entrepreneur and engineering leader who was previously CEO of Teleport Data, which was acquired by Fivetran in May 2021. By chance, Jason met Logan Smith, Senior Director of Corporate Business Development here at Grafana Labs, at an industry event two years ago, and had a conversation about Loki’s growing pains that planted a seed.

As Jason puts it: “After that conversation, I started thinking about new ways to do indexing over object storage. It took awhile, but eventually I had a breakthrough and thought, ‘wow, I may be onto something here—maybe Grafana Labs will be interested.’” 

With the acquisition, Logline brings a new indexing approach to Loki that’s designed specifically for high-cardinality attributes over object storage. Ultimately, this makes it much faster to find specific, highly unique values in large datasets, without changing Loki’s core design. 

“We want to drive down the time it takes to perform these searches without having to introduce techniques that are much more computationally expensive,” Jason says. “So Logline is the best of both worlds, where we can accelerate those needle-in-the-haystack searches with much simpler indexing than anything else that's on the market today.”

Early benchmarks being shared this week at GrafanaCON show that a query for a universally unique identifier (UUID) in Loki that previously scanned 3.5 TB of data without returning a result now scans just 8 GB with Logline—a 99.7% reduction in data scanned. 

These improvements, coupled with other major architectural changes coming to Loki, enable faster large-scale scans, minimize the impact of stream cardinality, and improve performance for analytical workloads.  

When it comes to Grafana Labs and Logline, the alignment goes far beyond technology. In addition to our shared commitment and appreciation for the open source community, Jason’s model for Logline mirrors our own goals of making observability easier, more accessible, and cost-effective for everyone.

"The mission of making observability ubiquitous while also keeping costs under control really stood out to me about Grafana Labs,” Jason recalls. “You don't usually see that in this space.”

Needle-in-the-haystack log queries are available in Grafana Cloud Logs in limited private preview today; please reach out to your sales rep to learn more. We are working to make them available to Loki OSS users in the coming year with the next major release of Loki.

To learn more about needle-in-the-haystack queries and Loki’s new architecture, be sure to check out the GrafanaCON 2026 opening keynote and Loki deep dive session when they become available on demand. 

In the meantime, we welcome Jason to Grafana Labs and into our wider open source community. 

For more information on this and all the other exciting updates from GrafanaCON 2026, check out our announcements blog post.

】

从一开始，Loki 就围绕一个简单的目标进行设计：使日志管理更具成本效益且更易于大规模操作。这种关注点就是团队继续转向开源、水平可扩展且高度可用的日志聚合系统的原因，该系统也一次又一次地为 Grafana Cloud Logs 提供支持。 

Loki 采用基于标签的索引方法，可保持较低的存储成本和轻量级的操作。对于大多数用例来说，这种设计效果非常好。但在大规模情况下，涉及高度唯一值（例如请求或作业 ID）的搜索可能会变得更具挑战性，因为查询需要更长的时间才能返回结果。 

随着我们的用户进入更大的数据集和要求更高的用例，我们希望扩展 Loki 的功能，同时又不影响其高效的成本和简单性。

因此，今天在 GrafanaCON 2026 上，我们很高兴与大家分享 Grafana Labs 收购了 Logline，专门从事“大海捞针”查询和全文搜索执行的公司。 

Logline 由 Jason Nochlin 创立，他是一位企业家和工程领导者，曾担任 Teleport Data 的首席执行官，该公司于 2021 年 5 月被 Fivetran 收购。两年前，Jason 在一次行业活动中偶然结识了 Grafana Labs 的企业业务开发高级总监 Logan Smith，并就 Loki 的成长烦恼进行了一次对话，从而播下了种子。

正如 Jason 所说：“那次谈话之后，我开始思考新的方法在对象存储上建立索引花了一段时间，但最终我取得了突破，我想，‘哇，我可能会感兴趣——也许 Grafana Labs 会感兴趣。’”

通过此次收购，Logline 为 Loki 带来了一种新的索引方法，该方法专为对象存储上的高基数属性而设计。最终，这使得在大型数据集中找到特定的、高度独特的值变得更快，而无需改变 Loki 的核心设计。 

“我们希望缩短执行这些搜索所需的时间，而不必引入计算成本更高的技术，”杰森说。 “因此，Logline 是两全其美的，我们可以通过比当今市场上任何其他产品更简单的索引来加速那些大海捞针的搜索。”

本周在 GrafanaCON 上共享的早期基准显示，在 Loki 中查询通用唯一标识符 (UUID) 时，之前会扫描 3.5 TB 数据而不返回结果，现在使用 Logline 只扫描 8 GB，减少了 99.7%在扫描的数据中。 

这些改进，再加上其他主要架构师Loki 的重大变化可以实现更快的大规模扫描，最大限度地减少流基数的影响，并提高分析工作负载的性能。  

当谈到 Grafana Labs 和 Logline 时，一致性远远超出了技术的范围。除了我们对开源社区的共同承诺和赞赏之外，Jason 的 Logline 模型也反映了我们自己的目标，即让每个人都更容易、更容易获得且更具成本效益的可观察性。

“让可观察性无处不在，同时控制成本，这一使命对我而言非常突出，”Jason 回忆道。 “您通常不会在这个领域看到这种情况。”

今天，Grafana Cloud Logs 中提供了大海捞针式日志查询的有限私人预览版；请联系您的销售代表以了解更多信息。我们正努力在来年通过 Loki 的下一个主要版本向 Loki OSS 用户提供这些功能。

要了解有关大海捞针查询和 Loki 新架构的更多信息，请务必查看 GrafanaCON 2026 开幕主题演讲和Loki 深入探讨课程（当它们按需提供时）。 

同时，我们欢迎 Jason 加入 Grafana Labs 并加入我们更广泛的开源社区。 

有关此内容以及 GrafanaCON 2026 的所有其他令人兴奋的更新的更多信息，请查看我们的公告博客文章。

item.title: 【GrafanaCON 2026 announcements: A guide to all the latest news from Grafana Labs】GrafanaCON 2026 公告：Grafana Labs 的所有最新消息指南
item.description:
【

GrafanaCON 2026 kicked off in Barcelona, which is a fitting city to reveal the latest updates in Grafana 13. 

In 2013, Grafana Labs Co-founder Torkel Ödegaard made the first commit for what would become Grafana while he was on vacation in the Catalan city. "I was traveling here for the Christmas holiday and I got a cold and spent most of the day in bed coding and working on Grafana," said Torkel during the opening keynote of GrafanaCON, our biggest community event of the year. "Even after I had gotten better, I stayed locked in my room working on Grafana … I was already obsessed with what I was building." 

Turns out, Grafana was infectious. The open source project now boasts more than 35 million users, including 100 Grafana champions, worldwide. 

"I want to reinforce that this journey has been our shared journey," said Grafana Labs Co-founder and CEO Raj Dutt while addressing the GrafanaCON audience. "We've gotten so much great feedback, input, pull requests, all of the above from so many people in the community, including many people in this room."

Today at GrafanaCON 2026, hundreds of those open source community members gathered to hear Grafana Labs' engineering and leadership teams deliver an action-packed opening keynote with announcements about how we're making it easier to get started with observability, easier to operate Grafana at scale, and easier to access and analyze your observability data from anywhere.

Here is a quick recap of all the major news coming out of GrafanaCON. Check out the full keynote to learn about the latest updates in open source observability updates and new developments in AI.

Grafana 13 release: From zero to insights in minutes 

The latest major release of Grafana is here to help you get value from your data faster than ever—whether you’re building dashboards, operating at scale, or evolving the platform to meet changing needs.

To get you from blank canvas to meaningful insights in just a few clicks, there are now suggested dashboards, which automatically surface pre-built dashboards tailored to your connected data sources with curated, ready-to-use visualization options. Dynamic dashboards, a dashboarding experience that is more intuitive, responsive, and scalable to better support growing teams, is also now generally available. Naturally, there are also new ways to visualize your data. (Did someone say Graphviz panel?)

Another way we're making it easier to spin up insights in Grafana? We announced that Grafana OSS and Grafana Enterprise users can now access Grafana Assistant, our AI-powered agent in Grafana Cloud, to further customize dashboards and templates, streamline SQL expressions, and more. (More on this below!) 

Grafana 13 is also focused on making it easier to maintain and operate Grafana at scale. Git Sync, now generally available, enables teams to manage observability as code by bringing native GitOps workflows into their Grafana instance. You can also now run regular health checks on your Grafana server and get actionable insights and recommendations for maintaining optimal system performance with the new Grafana Advisor tool. 

To learn more, check out our Grafana 13 blog post. 

Grafana Assistant everywhere

Where and how you run Grafana shouldn’t determine whether you can benefit from Grafana Assistant. That's why we're expanding the ways you can use and access our purpose-built LLM, including making Assistant available to our self-managed Grafana users.

If you're a Grafana OSS and Grafana Enterprise user, you can create a Grafana Cloud account and connect it to your Grafana installation via a one-click setup, giving you access to Assistant to help analyze telemetry data and code in real time, build dashboards, ask questions, and more. 

Customize Grafana Assistant to your needs

Use Assistant skills, now generally available, to create documents that give Assistant agents instructions, context, and specialized knowledge about your team's workflows. You can also use skills to write runbooks, connect to third-party tools, auto-approve tool calls, and set up auto-remediation pipelines. We've also added a new Assistant automations feature to generate regular summaries of activities so you can stay on top of everything in your instance. 

And to help reduce context switching and get closer to where you're already working, we've expanded the ways you can access Assistant, whether that's through Slack, Microsoft Teams, or via an API or CLI.
